title: Megascans/Fab/Bridge Terrain Assets

video.note: Students build a castle environment in Unreal Engine while recording daily screen/camera evidence for a making-of documentary.

learningTarget: I can import and place terrain assets responsibly while documenting how assets support the castle environment.

bellRinger.prompt: What makes an imported asset feel like it belongs in a scene instead of looking randomly placed?

vocabulary.definition: A dated record of what was built, what was captured, what changed, and what needs to happen next.
Video captured from the computer screen to show workflow, testing, or before/after changes.
Camera or screen-recorded evidence that shows how a project was made.
Supporting footage that helps explain a process, place, detail, or action.
The Q3 Unreal scene students build, refine, walk through, and document.

assignment.title: Terrain Asset Import and Placement
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of imported terrain assets placed in the scene.
Daily documentary clip showing an asset placement decision.

exitTicket: Which imported asset did you document, and why did it belong in your scene?
